_about: Data ingestion category (DataSource, Import, Integration, Blob, OrganizationUpload, Onboarding, Job, DistributedJobs, AdminDistributedJobs, AdminCrawler, AdminDeadLetterQueue, OrganizationAdminCrawler, OrganizationDataRemoval).

GET /api/v1/blob.description: Returns the decrypted content of a stored blob by id. If the blob is an image and `maxWidth`/`maxHeight` (or `size` with values `icon`, `small`, `medium`, `large`) are supplied, the image is resized server-side. The response sets `Content-Disposition`, `Content-Length`, `LastModified`, and `Content-MD5` headers when available. Returns 401 if the caller's claims do not permit the blob, 400 for invalid ids, 404 when the blob does not exist.

parameters.maxWidth: Maximum width in pixels (image blobs only).
parameters.maxHeight: Maximum height in pixels (image blobs only).

GET /api/v1/report.description: Returns the decrypted content of a blob as a text response, suitable for textual reports. Subject to the same authorization and 404 behaviour as `GET /api/v1/blob`.

GET /api/v1/integration/connector/all.description: Returns all available connectors (providers) for the organisation, optionally filtered by one or more `IntegrationType` values. Used by the UI integration browser.

parameters.integrationTypes: Optional list of integration types to filter by.

GET /api/v1/integration/connector/{id}.description: Returns a single connector by id. Returns 404 if the connector cannot be found.

POST /api/v1/integration/connector/basic.description: Returns lightweight metadata (id, name, icon, type) for the supplied list of connector ids. Used to render compact connector cards without fetching the full provider definition for each.

GET /api/v1/integration/connector/icon/{.description: Returns the icon asset for a connector. Returns 404 when the connector or its icon cannot be found.

POST /api/datasource/checkforupgrades.summary: Check whether data sources need upgrades
POST /api/datasource/checkforupgrades.description: Returns whether the organisation's data sources need migration/upgrades to a newer schema. Used as a pre-check before a platform upgrade.

POST /api/migration/import/import.description: Imports a zip archive of exported configuration (toolkit / migration export) via multipart upload. The caller must hold the Administration.Tenant RACI claim at Accountable level or higher (returns 403 otherwise). Returns 400 when the file is missing, empty, or not a valid zip archive, and 500 when `FrontEndGQLEndpoint` is not configured.

POST /api/organization/upload.description: Uploads CSV files via multipart-form upload (size limited to 10 MB per file). Each file is stored as an encrypted `Blob` and the response is a URL pointing at `GET /api/blob` for the uploaded blob.

GET /api/v1/onboarding/mostconnected.description: Returns the top 5 most-connected non-shadow entities in the organisation graph â€” typically used as suggestions on the onboarding/welcome dashboard.

GET /api/v1/jobs.description: Returns paged agent jobs filtered by `state` and an optional `configurationId` (provider definition).

GET /api/v1/jobs/executing.description: Returns the agent jobs currently in `Executing` state for the organisation.

GET /api/v1/jobstatecount.description: Returns counts of agent jobs grouped by state (`completed`, `executing`, `failed`, `queued`, `cancelled`).

GET /api/v1/jobrun.description: Returns a single agent job run by job id.

GET /api/v1/distributedJobs/progress/{id.description: Returns the current progress (percentage) and computed status (`InProgress`, `Stopping`, `Done`, `Failed`, `Aborted`) of a distributed job. Returns 400 if the job cannot be found.

POST /api/v1/distributedJobs/stop/{id}.description: Requests cancellation (abandonment) of a distributed job. Returns 404 when the job cannot be found.

GET /api/v1/admin/distributedJobs/runnin.description: Returns information for every distributed job currently running in the organisation. Admin-only.

GET /api/v1/admin/distributedJobs/info.description: Returns detailed information for a running distributed job. Returns 400 if the job id is not found. Admin-only.

POST /api/v1/admin/distributedJobs/aband.description: Requests abandonment of a distributed job and returns the prior progress state in the message. Returns 400 if the job is not found. Admin-only.

POST /api/v1/admin/distributedJobs/force.summary: Admin: force stale check for distributed job
POST /api/v1/admin/distributedJobs/force.description: Forces a stale-job check for the supplied job, optionally overriding the stale-update timeout in minutes. Returns whether the job was deemed stale and the resulting state. Admin-only.

parameters.staleJobUpdateTimeoutOverrideMinutes: Optional override in minutes for the stale-update timeout.

GET /api/v1/admincrawler.description: Returns the blob entity backing a crawler configuration as serialised XML. Optionally impersonates another organisation when `organizationId` is supplied. Returns 404 if the entity cannot be found. Requires the Administration.Provider RACI claim.

POST /api/v1/admincrawler/recrawl.description: Enqueues a recrawl job for a specific provider configuration in an organisation. Returns 500 when the organisation is inactive, the provider definition cannot be found, or job arguments fail to parse; 400 with details when the recrawl cannot be enqueued. Requires the Administration.Provider RACI claim.

POST /api/v1/admincrawler/recrawlall.summary: Admin: recrawl all configurations for a provider
POST /api/v1/admincrawler/recrawlall.description: Iterates every provider configuration for the supplied provider id (across organisations the caller can see) and enqueues a recrawl for each one. Resets `lastCrawlFinishTime`, `lastCursor`, and `firstTime` flags before enqueueing. Returns 500 when the provider is disabled or the configuration is invalid. Requires the Administration.Provider RACI claim.

POST /api/v1/admincrawler/runprovider.description: Placeholder for running a provider directly with a JSON payload. Currently throws `NotImplementedException`.

POST /api/v1/admin/deadletterqueue/repro.summary: Admin: reprocess processing dead-letter queue (legacy path)
POST /api/v1/admin/deadletterqueue/repro.description: Reprocesses messages from the clue-processing dead-letter queue. Legacy alias for `POST /api/v1/admin/deadLetterQueue/reprocess-processing`. Returns the summary `Status / Processed / Failed` counts. Requires the Administration.Messaging RACI claim.

parameters.limit: Optional cap on the number of messages to reprocess.

POST /api/v1/admin/deadLetterQueue/repro.description: Reprocesses messages from the clue-processing dead-letter queue, returning a status summary. Requires the Administration.Messaging RACI claim.

parameters.limit: Optional cap on the number of messages to reprocess.

POST /api/v1/admin/deadLetterQueue/repro.summary: Admin: reprocess global recoverable dead-letter queue
POST /api/v1/admin/deadLetterQueue/repro.description: Reprocesses messages from the global recoverable dead-letter queue, returning a status summary. Requires the Administration.Messaging RACI claim.

parameters.limit: Optional cap on the number of messages to reprocess.

POST /api/v1/admin/deadLetterQueue/repro.summary: Admin: reprocess global non-recoverable dead-letter queue
POST /api/v1/admin/deadLetterQueue/repro.description: Reprocesses messages from the global non-recoverable dead-letter queue, returning a status summary. Requires the Administration.Messaging RACI claim.

parameters.limit: Optional cap on the number of messages to reprocess.

POST /api/v1/organization/admin/provider.description: Enqueues a recrawl for a provider definition in the caller's organisation and logs an audit entry. Returns 400 for invalid ids, 404 if the provider definition cannot be found. Requires the Administration.Provider RACI claim.

POST /api/v1/organization/providers/recr.description: Triggers a recrawl of a provider configuration for the caller's organisation. Resets `lastCrawlFinishTime` and `lastCursor`, then schedules the crawl job. Returns 500 if the id is missing or invalid, the configuration is invalid, or the job server is unavailable; 404 if the provider configuration cannot be found. Requires the Administration.Provider RACI claim.
